items.title: Evaluating generalization in protein-ligand cofolding methods.
items.one_liner: Deep learning has driven major breakthroughs in protein structure prediction; however, one of the next critical steps forward is accurately predicting how proteins interact with small-molecule ligands, to enable real-world applications such as drug discovery. Recent cofolding methods aim to address this challenge, but evaluating their performance has been...

items.title: A hybrid transformer-GNN framework for social governance and urban service allocation.
items.one_liner: The proposed study puts forward an artificial intelligence-based framework to predict the needs of the urban public services and aid resource allocation based on data in the current social governance systems. A hybrid deep learning model is designed by combining a Graph Neural Network (GNN) based on spatial-relational reasoning with a Transformer network ...

items.title: Reducing social biases in text-based emotion prediction using semantic blinding and semantic propagation graph neural networks.
items.one_liner: This paper introduces the Semantic Propagation Graph Neural Network (SProp GNN), a machine learning emotion prediction (EP) architecture that relies exclusively on syntactic structures and word-level emotional cues to predict emotions in text. By semantically blinding the model to information about specific words, it is robust to social biases such as pol...

items.title: Mass Spectrometry-Based Proteomics Methods for Systematic Identification and Quantification of Protein O-Glycosylation in Complex Biological Samples.
items.source: PubMed — Journal of the American Society for Mass Spectrometry
items.one_liner: Protein O-glycosylation is one of the most common and important modifications in human cells. It regulates protein folding, trafficking, stability, and interactions with other molecules, and its dysregulation is directly related to numerous diseases such as cancer and neurodegenerative diseases. Modern mass spectrometry (MS)-based proteomics provides a un...

items.title: A de novo CO2 Reductase Featuring a Cysteine-Ligated Cobalt Porphyrin Cofactor
items.one_liner: Modern protein design methods based on deep learning allow generation of customized protein scaffolds with diverse geometries and functionalities. Here, we capitalize on these recent advances to develop hyper-thermostable de novo CO2 reductases featuring a cobalt porphyrin IX cofactor (CoPPIX). CoPPIX containing enzymes were assembled in vivo through medi...

items.title: De novo and scaffold-based design of GDF15 binders for cancer cachexia diagnostics and therapeutics.
items.one_liner: Growth differentiation factor-15 (GDF15), a stress-responsive cytokine of the transforming growth factor-β superfamily, is elevated in cancer cachexia, chemotherapy-induced nausea, and hyperemesis gravidarum, making it both a biomarker and a therapeutic target. Here, we developed high-affinity GDF15 binders using an artificial intelligence-driven protein ...

items.title: Molecular basis of BACE1 modulation revealed by machine learning, molecular simulations, and experimental validation.
items.source: PubMed — International journal of biological macromolecules
items.one_liner: Alzheimer's disease (AD) remains one of the most prevalent and debilitating neurodegenerative disorders worldwide, with no currently available disease-modifying treatments. β-site amyloid precursor protein cleaving enzyme 1 (BACE1) catalyzes the rate-limiting step in amyloid-β (Aβ) production and represents a validated therapeutic target for AD interventi...

items.title: SPRY domains encode ubiquitin ligase specificity for ZAP and RIG-I.
items.one_liner: Innate immune sensors rely on ubiquitin ligases to calibrate antiviral responses, yet the rules governing substrate recognition by SPRY-containing ligases remain poorly defined. Here, we establish a large-scale structure-based screening pipeline using AlphaFold to systematically predict interactions between human nucleic acid sensors and SPRY-containing p...

items.title: Artificial intelligence in the assessment of epilepsy-related genetic mutations: Learned from GABA
items.one_liner: This review examines how recent genetic and technological advances have transformed our understanding and treatment of genetic epilepsies (GEs), with a focus on disorders involving GABA

items.title: The World Health Organization Skin Neglected Tropical Diseases App: A dynamic capacity building training tool enhanced with artificial intelligence.
items.source: PubMed — The Journal of investigative dermatology
items.one_liner: Skin neglected tropical diseases (NTDs) remain a major public health challenge in low- and middle-income countries, where frontline health workers (FHWs) often lack dermatological training. In response, the World Health Organization (WHO) created the Skin NTDs App-developed by UniversalDoctor-to support FHWs in resource-limited settings. Initially created...

items.title: Predicting Enzyme pH Optima from Structure Using Equivariant Graph Neural Networks
items.one_liner: Enzyme activity and stability are strongly modulated by pH, making the catalytic pH optimum (pH opt) a key parameter in enzyme development and biotechnological applications. Experimental determination of pH opt is, however, labor-intensive and time-consuming, motivating the development of accurate computational prediction methods. Here, we introduce pHopt...

items.title: KG-Bench: Benchmarking Graph Neural Network Algorithms for Drug Repurposing.
items.one_liner: MOTIVATION: Drug repurposing leverages existing drugs for new indications, accelerating drug development. Computational methods integrating diverse biological and chemical data can systematically prioritize repurposing candidates, but standardized benchmarks for deep learning evaluation are lacking. We present KG-Bench, a GNN benchmarking framework design...

items.title: D614G reshapes allosteric networks and opening mechanisms of SARS-CoV-2 spikes.
items.source: PubMed — Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
items.one_liner: The severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 spike glycoprotein enables infection through a key conformational transition that exposes its receptor binding domain (RBD). Experimental evidence indicates that spike mutations, particularly the early D614G variant, alter the rate of this conformational shift, potentially increasing viral infectivity. W...

items.title: The latest AI breakthroughs in structural biology: protein binder design and conformational state prediction.
items.one_liner:
AI‑driven tools such as Microsoft’s BioEmu and BindCraft are delivering an “AlphaFold moment” for protein conformational landscape prediction and de novo binder design, enabling rapid sampling of Boltzmann‑weighted states and high‑affinity binders without extensive experimental screening.

METHOD / APPROACH:
- BioEmu trains a generative diffusion model on PDB.

items.title: Structural Basis for HIV-1 Rev Recognition by the Histone Chaperone Human Nap1.
items.source: PubMed — The Journal of biological chemistry
items.one_liner: Human Nap1 (hNap1) is a histone chaperone involved in chromatin dynamics and has been shown to interact with the HIV-1 regulatory protein Rev, which is essential for nuclear export of viral RNA. Despite the functional significance of this interaction, its structural basis has remained elusive. Here, we present the X-ray crystal structure of hNap1 and the ...

items.title: NeuroPred-GMC: a dual-branch deep learning architecture for neuropeptide prediction based on gated dilated convolutional network and multi-scale convolutional network.
items.source: PubMed — Journal of computer-aided molecular design
items.one_liner: Neuropeptides are multifunctional signaling molecules in the nervous system. By modulating synaptic transmission and integrating physiological systems, they influence a broad range of functions from pain perception to emotional regulation. Predicting neuropeptides can rapidly expand the library of potential therapeutic targets, thereby providing novel can...

items.title: Flux sampling and graph neural networks for improved gene essentiality prediction in mammalian genome-scale metabolic models.
items.source: PubMed — NPJ systems biology and applications
items.one_liner: Gene essentiality, the requirement of a gene for survival or proliferation, is central to understanding cellular processes and identifying drug targets. Experimental determination requires large growth screens that are time-consuming and expensive, motivating in silico approaches. Existing methods predominantly use flux balance analysis (FBA), a constrain...

items.title: Graph Rotation Network: Equivariant Graph Neural Network for Efficient Inverse Design in 4-D Printing.
items.source: PubMed — IEEE transactions on neural networks and learning systems
items.one_liner: Four-dimensional printed structures transform shape under external stimuli, and designing these structures to achieve desired deformation outcomes is crucial for 4-D printing technology. Current machine-learning (ML)-assisted methods can design 4-D-printed structures rapidly and accurately, but often overlook the E(3) equivariance required for the model, ...

items.title: Capricor sues Nippon Shinyaku over Duchenne drug 'pricing flaw' and launch prep
items.one_liner: Capricor Therapeutics said Thursday it has filed a lawsuit against Nippon Shinyaku over a distribution and commercialization agreement for deramiocel, a cell therapy candidate for Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) that’s getting a second chance at ...

items.title: Daiichi Sankyo posts 'extraordinary loss' of nearly $1B
items.one_liner:
Daiichi Sankyo is scrapping plans to build antibody-drug conjugate manufacturing capacity and recorded an "extraordinary loss" of 149.4 billion Japanese yen ($950 million) after overpromising demand for its ADCs to third-party manufacturers.

 The Japanese company ...
